title: known MVP - Memory Graph for LLMs
description: Build a memory graph system for LLMs with: vector similarity search, graph traversal, hierarchical scopes, conflict detection, and LLM-first resolution. PostgreSQL+pgvector backend, pluggable embeddings (local-first), CLI interface.
design:
## Architectural Decisions

### Module Structure
- Single Go module: github.com/dustin/known (or similar)
- Packages: model/, storage/, embed/, query/, cmd/

### Configuration
- Viper for configuration
- Config file: ~/.known/config.yaml
- Environment variable overrides

### IDs
- ULID for all entity IDs (sortable, URL-safe)

### Embedding Dimensions
- Stored per-entry to support mixed embedding models
- Allows migration between models without re-embedding everything

### Predefined Edge Types
- depends-on, contradicts, supersedes, elaborates, related-to
- Extensible via edge_types table

### Scope Model
- Hierarchical paths (e.g., 'project.auth.oauth')
- Scoped queries stay within scope unless explicitly traversing links
- No implicit parent inheritance

### Conflict Resolution
- LLM-first: surface conflicts with metadata for calling model to decide
- Stable confidence metadata (source-based, not LLM-generated)
- ResolutionStatus: unresolved | resolved | superseded

title: Core Knowledge Model
description: Define Go structs for the knowledge graph primitives
design:
## Data Structures

### Entry (knowledge unit)
- ID (ULID)
- Content (text, user-defined granularity)
- Embedding ([]float32 + dimension)
- Source (provenance: file path, URL, conversation ID, etc.)
- SourceMeta (line numbers, timestamps, etc.)
- Confidence (stable metadata, not LLM-generated)
- CreatedAt, UpdatedAt
- TTL (optional expiration)
- Scope (hierarchical path)
- Custom metadata (extensible key-value)

### Edge (relationship)
- ID (ULID)
- FromID, ToID (ULID refs)
- Type (predefined + extensible)
- Weight (optional)
- Metadata (extensible)
- CreatedAt

### Predefined Edge Types
- depends-on: X requires Y
- contradicts: X conflicts with Y (for conflict detection)
- supersedes: X replaces Y (newer version)
- elaborates: X provides detail about Y
- related-to: generic association

### Scope
- Path (hierarchical, e.g., 'project.auth.oauth')
- Inheritance rules: queries stay scoped unless explicitly following links

## Conflict Metadata
- ConflictsWith []EntryID
- ResolutionStatus: unresolved | resolved | superseded
- LastVerified timestamp
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Go structs defined: Entry, Edge, Scope, EdgeType
- [ ] ULID generation for all IDs
- [ ] Predefined edge types as typed constants (depends-on, contradicts, supersedes, elaborates, related-to)
- [ ] Extensible metadata field (map[string]any or typed wrapper)
- [ ] Embedding dimension stored per-entry (supports mixed models)
- [ ] JSON serialization/deserialization working
- [ ] Validation methods (e.g., scope path format, required fields)
- [ ] Unit tests for all structs and validation
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met. Core model implemented with Entry, Edge, Scope, EdgeType structs. ULID-based IDs, predefined edge types, extensible metadata, per-entry embedding dimensions, JSON serialization, and validation methods. 30 unit tests passing.

title: Storage Layer - PostgreSQL + pgvector
description: Implement PostgreSQL storage with pgvector for embeddings and ltree for hierarchical scopes
design:
## Schema Design

### Tables
- entries: core knowledge storage with vector column
- edges: relationship graph
- scopes: scope metadata and configuration
- edge_types: registry of valid edge types (predefined + custom)

### Key Features
- pgvector for similarity search (cosine, L2, inner product)
- ltree for hierarchical scope paths
- GIN indexes for metadata JSONB queries
- Partial indexes for active (non-expired) entries

## Interface
- Repository pattern with interfaces for testability
- Connection pooling (pgxpool)
- Migration support (golang-migrate or similar)

## Queries to Support
- Vector similarity within scope
- Graph traversal (BFS/DFS)
- Conflict detection (find contradicts edges)
- TTL expiration cleanup
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PostgreSQL schema with migrations (golang-migrate)
- [ ] pgvector column for embeddings (dimension from entry)
- [ ] ltree column for hierarchical scope paths
- [ ] Repository interfaces: EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, ScopeRepo
- [ ] CRUD operations for all entities
- [ ] Vector similarity search (cosine) within scope
- [ ] Basic adjacency queries (edges in/out)
- [ ] TTL expiration cleanup function
- [ ] Connection pooling with pgxpool
- [ ] Integration tests with testcontainers

title: Embedding Layer - Pluggable Local-First
description: Create pluggable embedding interface with local-first implementation (Ollama) and OpenAI-compatible adapter
design:
## Interface Design

type Embedder interface {
    Embed(ctx context.Context, text string) ([]float32, error)
    EmbedBatch(ctx context.Context, texts []string) ([][]float32, error)
    Dimensions() int
    ModelName() string
}

## Implementations

### OllamaEmbedder (default, local-first)
- Uses Ollama API (localhost:11434)
- Supports models: nomic-embed-text, mxbai-embed-large, etc.
- Configurable model selection

### OpenAICompatibleEmbedder
- Works with any OpenAI-compatible API
- Configurable base URL, API key, model
- Supports: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, local servers (vLLM, etc.)

## Configuration
- KNOWN_EMBEDDER=ollama|openai-compatible
- KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL=nomic-embed-text
- KNOWN_EMBED_URL=http://localhost:11434
- KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY=(for remote APIs)

## Caching
- Optional embedding cache to avoid re-embedding identical content
- Content hash → embedding mapping
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Embedder interface defined (Embed, EmbedBatch, Dimensions, ModelName)
- [ ] OllamaEmbedder implementation working
- [ ] OpenAICompatibleEmbedder implementation working
- [ ] Viper configuration for embedder selection and settings
- [ ] Returns dimension with embeddings for storage
- [ ] Graceful error handling (connection failures, model not found)
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked HTTP responses
- [ ] Integration test with real Ollama (skippable if not available)

title: Query Engine - Vector + Graph + Hybrid
description: Implement query engine supporting vector similarity, graph traversal, and hybrid queries with conflict detection
design:
## Query Types

### Vector Search
- Similarity search within scope
- Top-K with threshold
- Recency weighting option
- Exclusion filters (NOT containing X)

### Graph Traversal  
- Directional: outgoing edges (what does X depend on?)
- Directional: incoming edges (what depends on X?)
- Transitive closure (N hops from X)
- Filter by edge type
- Path finding between entries

### Hybrid Queries
- Vector search + graph expansion
- 'Find similar to X, then expand relationships'
- 'Find everything in scope related to topic within N hops'

### Conflict Detection
- Find entries with 'contradicts' edges
- Surface unresolved conflicts for LLM decision
- Return conflict metadata with query results

## Result Format
- Entry with full metadata
- Provenance chain (source info)
- Conflict flags
- Relevance/similarity scores
- Relationship context (how was this reached?)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Vector similarity search with scope filtering and top-K
- [ ] Recency weighting option for similarity results
- [ ] Exclusion filters (NOT matching X)
- [ ] Graph traversal: outgoing edges from entry
- [ ] Graph traversal: incoming edges to entry
- [ ] Transitive closure (N hops) with edge type filtering
- [ ] Hybrid query: vector search + graph expansion
- [ ] Conflict detection: surface entries with contradicts edges
- [ ] Results include: entry, provenance, similarity score, conflict flags, path info
- [ ] Integration tests for all query types

title: CLI Interface
description: Build CLI tool for interacting with the knowledge graph (similar to beads UX)
design:
## Commands

### Knowledge Management
- known add 'content' --scope=X --source=Y --tags=a,b
- known update <id> --content=... --confidence=...
- known delete <id>
- known show <id> (full entry with relationships)

### Querying
- known search 'query' --scope=X --limit=10
- known related <id> --depth=2 --edge-type=depends-on
- known conflicts [--scope=X] (show unresolved conflicts)

### Graph Operations
- known link <from> <to> --type=depends-on
- known unlink <from> <to>
- known path <from> <to> (find connection)

### Scope Management
- known scope list
- known scope create <path>
- known scope tree (show hierarchy)

### Maintenance
- known gc (clean expired entries)
- known stats [--scope=X]
- known export --format=json|jsonl
- known import <file>

## Output Formats
- Human-readable (default)
- JSON (--json flag)
- Quiet mode for scripting (--quiet)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Commands: add, update, delete, show (knowledge management)
- [ ] Commands: search, related, conflicts (querying)
- [ ] Commands: link, unlink, path (graph operations)
- [ ] Commands: scope list/create/tree (scope management)
- [ ] Commands: gc, stats, export, import (maintenance)
- [ ] Viper config file support (~/.known/config.yaml)
- [ ] --json flag for machine-readable output
- [ ] --quiet flag for scripting
- [ ] Helpful error messages and --help for all commands
- [ ] Basic integration tests for key workflows
